title: Refine comment type contract to human or system
description:
Why: consumers want a smaller, clearer normalized comment contract with only human and system values instead of multiple user/change/unknown-style categories.

Scope:
- Change normalized ticket comment type values to human or system.
- Document the mapping and fallback behavior.
- Update tests covering representative comment classifications.
close_reason: Refined normalized comment type contract to human or system, updated docs, and adjusted classification tests.

title: Omit embedded comments from tickets.get
description:
Why: tickets.get should stay focused on ticket fields and avoid returning raw embedded discussion posts that duplicate the dedicated comments endpoint.

Scope:
- Stop including embedded discussion posts in tickets.get output.
- Preserve ticket-level discussion metadata needed for follow-up comment fetches.
- Update docs and tests to reflect that comments are fetched explicitly via tickets.comments.
close_reason: Removed embedded discussion posts from tickets.get while preserving thread metadata and documenting explicit comment fetching.

title: Add activity_type contract for ticket activity entries
description:
Why: Ticket activity feeds are hard to consume when activity kinds must be inferred from sparse fields. A first-class activity_type contract will make activity processing predictable.

Scope:
- Define the supported activity_type values for ticket activity entries.
- Ensure activity entries expose activity_type consistently.
- Document mapping rules for provider-specific activity events.
acceptance_criteria:
- Ticket activity entries include a documented activity_type contract.
- Supported activity_type values and meanings are documented.
- Mapping or fallback behavior for provider-specific events is documented.
- Tests cover representative activity events and activity_type assignments.
close_reason: Implemented activity_type ticket activity contract, documentation, and representative classification tests.

title: Classify comment types in normalized ticket comments output
description:
Why: Downstream consumers need a stable way to distinguish user-authored comments from system-generated notes or other comment variants without inferring intent from raw text.

Scope:
- Define the supported comment type categories for normalized ticket comments output.
- Ensure comment records expose the chosen classification consistently.
- Document how unknown or provider-specific comment forms are handled.
acceptance_criteria:
- Normalized comment output includes a documented type/classification field or equivalent contract.
- At least user vs system comment behavior is explicitly defined.
- Unknown or unmapped comment forms have a documented fallback behavior.
- Tests cover representative comment classification cases.
close_reason: Implemented normalized ticket comment type classification, docs, and representative tests.

title: Define canonical ticket schema contract and add conformance tests
description:
Why: Consumers need one canonical ticket schema they can rely on across commands and transports, and the project needs tests that lock that contract in place.

Scope:
- Define the canonical ticket payload shape and required/optional fields.
- Specify field semantics, nullability, and compatibility expectations.
- Add automated tests that fail on contract drift.
acceptance_criteria:
- Canonical ticket schema is documented in a source-of-truth location.
- Required, optional, and nullable fields are explicitly identified.
- Automated tests verify the canonical ticket schema contract and fail on incompatible changes.
- Documentation explains how future schema changes should be introduced compatibly.
close_reason: Defined the canonical ticket schema contract, documented compatibility rules, and added conformance tests for list/search/get outputs.

title: Normalize JSON envelope contract
description: Define and implement one canonical JSON success/error envelope contract for all sf-cli commands so callers can depend on stable top-level fields, error shapes, and warning/reporting semantics instead of command-specific variations.
acceptance_criteria: All commands share the same documented top-level envelope keys and error structure, warning semantics are explicit, and tests cover representative success and failure responses across the CLI.
close_reason: Normalized the CLI JSON envelope contract with explicit top-level warnings, stable error fields, updated docs, and representative passing tests.

title: Normalize comments output and ordering
description: Standardize the comments command so comment objects use a stable schema and deterministic ordering, avoiding ambiguity about chronology, identifiers, authorship, and empty or missing fields.
acceptance_criteria: Comments output uses one documented object shape, ordering is explicit and test-covered, and edge cases such as missing timestamps or empty threads preserve deterministic results.
close_reason: Normalized comments output to a stable comment shape, made ordering deterministic, documented the contract, and added regression tests for ordering and empty-thread cases.

title: Normalize ticket outputs across list, search, and get
description: Align ticket payloads returned by tickets list, tickets search, and tickets get so equivalent ticket fields use the same names, shapes, nullability rules, and derived metadata regardless of which read path produced them.
acceptance_criteria: List, search, and get return a documented common ticket shape for overlapping fields, differences are intentional and documented, and regression tests verify consistent output across the three commands.
close_reason: Normalized overlapping ticket fields across list, search, and get, documented the shared ticket contract, and added regression coverage for cross-command consistency.

title: Normalize API output contracts and validation metadata
description: Plan and deliver the next iteration of sf-cli output-contract work after the MVP. This iteration should make JSON responses more predictable across envelopes, ticket resources, comments, pagination, tracker schema metadata, and validation tooling so downstream automation can rely on stable structured output.
acceptance_criteria: A parent issue exists for the next iteration, child issues cover the agreed normalization sequence, and the plan clearly rolls up the contract work needed for implementation and validation.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Add tests for envelope auth and ticket workflows
description: Cover the CLI with automated tests using the Go standard library, including JSON envelope formatting, bearer auth propagation, and ticket list/search/get/comments behavior plus schema edge cases. Acceptance is repeatable go test coverage for the MVP command surface without external service dependencies.
close_reason: Added repeatable tests for JSON envelope formatting, CLI bearer token propagation, ticket workflows, and tracker schema edge cases; go test and go build pass.

title: Implement ticket list and search commands
description: Add commands to list tickets and search tickets through Allura APIs using the shared client and output envelope. Acceptance is testable support for listing and searching tickets with stable JSON output and error handling for API failures.
close_reason: Implemented API-backed tickets list/search commands with JSON envelopes, argument validation, and passing tests/build.

title: Implement ticket get and comments commands
description: Add commands to fetch a single ticket and retrieve ticket comments using the shared authenticated client. Acceptance is testable JSON output for ticket detail and comments retrieval, including not-found and API error cases.
close_reason: Implemented API-backed tickets get/comments commands with argument validation, thread lookup, and passing tests/build.

title: Bootstrap stdlib Go CLI foundation with JSON envelope and bearer auth
description: Set up the Go CLI using only the standard library, including command dispatch, shared JSON-only success/error envelope, HTTP client wiring, and bearer token authentication handling. Acceptance is a buildable CLI skeleton with authenticated request plumbing and no third-party CLI/runtime dependencies.
close_reason: Bootstrapped stdlib Go CLI foundation with JSON envelope, bearer auth plumbing, --token/SF_BEARER_TOKEN support, and passing tests/build.

title: Build SourceForge Allura Go CLI MVP
description: Create a standard-library Go CLI for SourceForge Allura with JSON-only output envelopes and bearer token authentication. Scope includes tickets list/search/get/comments, partial best-effort tracker schema support, and test/build verification; explicitly excludes docker runner support and repository clone/fetch functionality.
close_reason: MVP scope completed across child work: stdlib CLI foundation, tickets list/search/get/comments, best-effort tracker schema, README/help support, and passing test/build verification.

title: Document canonical ticket contract directly in README
description:
Why: The README should describe the canonical ticket contract directly for users instead of sending them to an internal source file.

Scope:
- Replace the README reference to internal/cli/ticket_contract.go with direct contract documentation.
- Keep the required/optional/nullability and compatibility guidance aligned with the implemented contract.
close_reason: Documented the canonical ticket contract directly in README instead of referring readers to an internal source file.

title: Clarify envelope semantics for result, proposal, and warnings fields
description:
Why: The response envelope needs unambiguous guidance so consumers know when data belongs in result, when proposal is appropriate, and how warnings should be interpreted without guessing.

Scope:
- Define the intended semantics of result, proposal, and warnings in the response envelope.
- Clarify when fields may appear together versus when one should be absent.
- Provide examples that show the expected envelope shape for common cases.
acceptance_criteria:
- Documentation clearly defines result, proposal, and warnings semantics.
- Rules for coexistence, absence, and precedence are documented.
- Examples cover successful results, proposal-driven responses, and warning-bearing responses.
- Guidance is specific enough that implementers can produce and validate envelopes consistently.
close_reason: Implemented envelope field semantics docs and tests

title: Document pagination ordering and continuity guarantees for ticket APIs
description:
Why: Clients need to know whether paginated ticket results are stable, how ordering works, and what continuity guarantees exist when fetching multiple pages over changing data.

Scope:
- Document the ordering contract for paginated ticket results.
- Define any continuity guarantees or explicit non-guarantees across page boundaries.
- Clarify how clients should handle inserts, updates, or duplicates while paginating.
acceptance_criteria:
- Documentation states the default sort/order for paginated ticket results.
- Continuity guarantees or caveats across page boundaries are explicitly documented.
- Guidance covers duplicate, missing, or shifted records during pagination.
- If the system provides stronger guarantees, they are stated clearly enough for clients to depend on them.

title: Document identifier consistency contract across ticket representations
description:
Why: Clients need a clear contract for which identifiers are canonical, where they remain stable, and how the same ticket should be matched across list, detail, comments, and activity surfaces.

Scope:
- Document the canonical identifier(s) for tickets and related entities.
- Clarify consistency expectations across list/detail/activity/comment representations.
- Explain any provider-specific IDs, aliases, or normalization rules.
acceptance_criteria:
- Documentation names the canonical ticket identifier contract.
- Cross-surface consistency expectations are documented for list, detail, comments, and activity outputs.
- Provider-specific or secondary identifiers are distinguished from canonical identifiers.
- Guidance includes how clients should correlate records across surfaces.

title: Add compact field selection for ticket reads
description: Add field projection support such as --fields id,title,status,updated_at for read commands so agents and automation can request compact payloads with less noise and lower token cost. Start with ticket-oriented reads where the response shape is currently largest and most repetitive.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover representative compact and invalid field-selection cases.
notes: Requested by user, but do not start yet.
close_reason: Added --fields compact projection support across read/query commands, with tests and docs.

title: Replace tickets page-based pagination contract with cursor-based contract
description: The current tickets pagination contract is page-based. We want to replace it with a cursor-based contract and the user is explicitly happy to replace the existing contract rather than preserve backward compatibility. Define and implement the contract change so consumers can move to cursor-based pagination cleanly.
acceptance_criteria: Tickets pagination contract uses cursor-based request/response fields instead of page-based fields; Existing page-based contract is removed/replaced rather than preserved in parallel; Any impacted callers/tests/docs are updated to the new cursor-based contract and verified
notes: User confirmed they are happy to replace the existing contract.
close_reason: Replaced the tickets page-based pagination contract with a cursor-based public contract, updated flags and response metadata, and revised tests/docs accordingly.

title: Add tickets activity read command
description: Add a read-only 'tickets activity' command so users can inspect ticket activity/history from the CLI. This issue captures the request to define and implement the command behavior and output in a way that fits the existing tickets command surface.
acceptance_criteria: A 'tickets activity' read command is defined in the CLI command surface.|The command returns ticket activity/history without mutating ticket state.|Documentation or help text makes the command purpose and usage clear.
close_reason: Added a tickets activity read command that returns recently active tickets with last comment metadata, plus help/docs and regression tests.

title: Add explicit pagination contract
description: Introduce a first-class pagination contract for commands that return collections so clients can reliably discover page boundaries, cursors or offsets, total-count semantics, and truncation behavior without inferring them from raw upstream responses.
acceptance_criteria: Paginated responses expose documented pagination metadata, absence of additional pages is unambiguous, and tests cover first-page, middle-page, final-page, and unpaginated collection scenarios.
close_reason: Added an explicit pagination contract for paginated collection responses, documented the normalized metadata, and covered first, middle, final, and unpaginated cases with tests.

title: Expand tracker schema into structured validation metadata
description: Evolve tracker schema output from best-effort raw metadata into structured validation-oriented metadata that callers can use to understand field types, requiredness, allowed values, defaults, and other input constraints.
acceptance_criteria: Tracker schema output includes documented structured validation metadata where upstream data permits, partial or missing upstream information is represented predictably, and tests cover representative schema field variations.
close_reason: Expanded tracker schema output with structured field validation metadata, including inferred allowed values and milestone defaults, plus regression coverage for representative schema variations.

title: Add actions validate dry-run command
description: Add a dry-run validation command for write-oriented actions so users and automation can verify payloads against the normalized envelope and tracker validation contracts before attempting a mutating request.
acceptance_criteria: A validate dry-run command exists for the targeted action flow, returns normalized machine-readable validation results without side effects, and tests cover valid, invalid, and partially constrained inputs.
close_reason: Added a dry-run actions validate command for file-based ticket comment intents, returning machine-readable validation results without side effects and covering valid, invalid, and warning-only cases with tests.

title: Add CLI help and usage support
description: The CLI exposes commands but does not yet provide standard user-facing help output for discovering available commands, arguments, and authentication options. Add built-in help and usage text so users can understand how to invoke the CLI without external documentation.
acceptance_criteria: Top-level and subcommand help is available via standard help entry points, documents arguments and authentication options, and invalid or incomplete invocations return actionable usage guidance.
close_reason: Added root and subcommand help support via standard help entry points and actionable usage guidance for incomplete invocations.

title: Add top-level README.md for end users
description: The CLI currently lacks a top-level README that explains what the tool is for, how to install or build it, how to configure authentication, and how to run the supported commands. Add user-facing documentation that makes the MVP discoverable and usable without reading the source.
acceptance_criteria: Repository root contains a README.md that explains purpose, prerequisites, build/install steps, authentication setup, and example usage for the MVP commands, and it clearly states current scope and limitations.
close_reason: Added a top-level README.md covering build, auth, help, commands, JSON output, and MVP scope/limits.

title: Add project tools discovery command
description: Add a read-only CLI command to fetch a project's tool mounts from the Allura project endpoint so users can discover valid tracker names like bugs or feature-requests. Acceptance is a JSON-only command with tests covering successful decoding and argument validation.
close_reason: Added project.tools command to discover Allura tool mounts with JSON output and tests.

title: Verify clean build and release-ready CLI workflow
description: Add a final verification milestone for building and testing the CLI in a clean local workflow. Acceptance is documented and runnable go test and go build verification for the scoped MVP, excluding docker runner support and repo clone/fetch work.
close_reason: Documented runnable MVP verification commands in CLAUDE.md and confirmed go test ./... plus go build ./... pass.

title: Add partial best-effort tracker schema support
description: Expose tracker schema metadata as a partial, best-effort capability that tolerates missing or inconsistent upstream fields. Acceptance is documented JSON output that returns available schema information without blocking core ticket operations when schema data is incomplete.
close_reason: Implemented tracker schema command with best-effort partial metadata, warnings for missing upstream schema endpoints, and passing tests/build.

title: Improve tickets list query error
description: Return a clearer JSON invalid-arguments error when users pass --query to tickets list, directing them to tickets search instead of exposing the raw flag parser message. Acceptance is covered by tests.
close_reason: Improved tickets list invalid-argument guidance for --query and added regression coverage.
